# Only Once Share — ooshare.io

> Free, open-source, end-to-end encrypted secret and image sharing tool.

Only Once Share lets you send passwords, images, API keys, and sensitive information securely via one-time self-destructing links. Secrets are encrypted in the browser using AES-256-GCM before transmission — the server never sees plaintext data.

## Key Facts

- **Encryption**: AES-256-GCM with HKDF-SHA-256 key derivation (client-side, Web Crypto API)
- **Architecture**: Zero-knowledge — the server stores only encrypted blobs
- **Image Sharing**: Drag-and-drop encrypted image sharing (JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP up to 10 MB)
- **Content Types**: Text only, image only, or text + image together in one secret
- **Pricing**: Completely free, no limits, no account required
- **License**: MIT open source (https://github.com/dhdtech/only-once-share)
- **Deployment**: Cloud at ooshare.io or self-hosted via Docker
- **Languages**: English, Chinese, Spanish, Hindi, Arabic, Portuguese
- **TTL Options**: 1, 4, 12, 24, 48, or 72 hours
- **URL Format**: 8-character base62 short aliases

## How It Works

1. User enters secret text and/or drops an image in the browser
2. Browser generates a random AES-256-GCM key via Web Crypto API
3. Text and image are packed into a binary envelope and encrypted entirely client-side
4. The encryption key is placed in the URL fragment (#) — never sent to any server
5. Only the encrypted blob is sent to the server and stored in Redis with a TTL
6. A one-time link is generated containing the encrypted secret ID and the key in the fragment
7. When the recipient opens the link, the encrypted blob is retrieved and atomically deleted (Redis GETDEL)
8. The browser decrypts the secret and displays text and/or image
9. The secret can never be viewed again

## Image Sharing Feature

Only Once Share is the only secret sharing tool that combines encrypted image sharing with zero-knowledge architecture and one-time viewing. Unlike competitors (OneTimeSecret, Privnote, scrt.link, Password.link) which only support text, Only Once Share encrypts images client-side with the same AES-256-GCM standard.

- Drag-and-drop or click to upload images
- Supports JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP up to 10 MB
- Images are encrypted in the browser before upload — the server never sees the original image
- View images in a secure modal — no download button, no right-click save
- Send text + image together in one self-destructing link

## What Makes It Different

- True zero-knowledge: encryption key never leaves the browser (URL fragments are never sent to servers)
- Only tool combining encrypted image sharing + zero-knowledge + one-time viewing
- Completely free with no artificial limits (unlike OneTimeSecret's 100KB cap or Password.link's 8/month)
- Open source MIT license — fully auditable code
- 6-language support for global accessibility
- No account required — zero friction
